front: Composer: directed the Eastman School of Music from 1924 to 1964; hosted the NBC radio series Pioneers in Music to spotlight American composers
Composer: 1960 treatise Harmonic Materials of Modern Music introduced isomeric (Z-)relations, laying groundwork for musical set theory before Allen Forte
Composer: wrote a 'Nordic' First Symphony and a seventh symphony setting three ocean-themed Walt Whitman poems
Symphony: nicknamed 'Romantic'; in D-flat major; its Interlochen theme (strings: long G, triplet G–Ab–Bb, long G) ends all Interlochen Arts Camp concerts
Symphony: used without permission in the closing credits of Alien (1979); the composer decided not to sue 20th Century Fox
Symphony: commissioned by Serge Koussevitzky for the 50th anniversary of the Boston Symphony; partly composed while teaching at Interlochen Arts Camp
Symphony: John Williams cited it as an inspiration for his score for E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial
Opera: Hanson's only opera; its concert suite includes a love duet between Marigold and Bradford and a 'Maypole Dance'
